scores.note: Floor 6 stable across 3 evidence-driven C-exhaustion sessions (50+ structural variants + ~50k permuter iters total).

sessions.lever: session 15 (round 11): tested ONE genuinely-novel structural variant NOT in any prior rejected_form — `mask = 0xFFFFFF; *ot = ((u32)&g_gpu_ot_end) & mask;` (FUSED-AND-INTO-STORE while keeping Lever B's const-first mask init statement). Distinct from rejected_forms[0] (which fuses BOTH mask= statements into `mask = addr & 0xFFFFFF`) — this round keeps mask=0xFFFFFF as a separate statement and only eliminates the second `mask = addr & mask` rebinding by inlining the AND into the store's RHS.
sessions.note: NO_PROGRESS. Variant REGRESSED 6→7 (build_insns 38, target_insns 38 — masked diff +1 vs candidate). Mechanism: removing the `mask = ((u32)&g_gpu_ot_end) & mask;` REBINDING statement removes the named-pseudo target for the AND result — cc1 allocates the AND result to a fresh pseudo (not `mask`'s register), which breaks the addr-register $v1 alignment that Lever B established. Confirms that the named-intermediate REBINDING (not just the named-intermediate's existence) is what holds Lever B's specific addr-register alignment. The mask-init statement (LUID 5/6 for li+ori) is necessary but NOT sufficient — the rebinding statement (LUID 7/8 for la+and going INTO mask's pseudo) is ALSO load-bearing. Joins the score-7 rejected-forms cluster (single-statement mask fuse, val-split, split-name addr-then-AND-in-store, round-10 reverse order, this round fused-AND-into-store) — all five variants share the broken-Lever-B-alignment failure mode at the AND result's register allocation. Together they pin Lever B's structure as TIGHTLY constrained: exactly 3 statements (mask=CONST; mask=ADDR&mask; *ot=mask;) with no fusion, no reordering, no rebinding-elimination tolerated. CUMULATIVE EVIDENCE across 13 grinding contexts (3 sessions + 2 workflow rounds + rounds 3-11): ~85+ structural variants, ~50.5k+ permuter iters, ZERO progress beyond floor 6. The mechanical ceiling (sched.c INSN_PRIORITY 1 vs 4 wall, per round-8's ALLOCDBG/SCHEDDBG/PRIODBG instrumented dump) stands. Per .claude/rules/no-new-park-categories.md standing posture: continue indefinite WIP. NEXT-SESSION un-attempted concrete actions (carried forward from rounds 7/8/9/10): (1) BB2_SCHED_DEBUG dump on the &ot Angle-1 form to characterise the schedule cascade; (2) gpu_SendPacket sibling .sched dump cross-reference; (3) directed-PERM_GENERAL permuter with explicit `// PERM_GENERAL(stmts)` macro annotations around post-call statements (still un-attempted at the PERM_GENERAL spelling layer).

Re-verified score-6 baseline at HEAD fa269789. Tested 7 NEW structural variants AT the score-6 base (none in rejected_forms; all derived this session) AND ran a 12-min randomization permuter from the score-6 base. ALL NEGATIVE.
sessions.note: NO_PROGRESS. Per user instruction (orchestrator brief 2026-06-04) — SOTN-research escalation from round 5 was OVERRIDDEN; this round was directed to continue grinding with permuter authorized. Re-confirmed score-6 baseline. Seven NEW structural variants tested at score-6 base (variants NOT in rejected_forms): (v1) assign-in-return-comma form `return (*ot = ((u32)&g_gpu_ot_end) & mask, ot);` — REGRESSED 6→7 (the assignment-in-return-context loses Lever B's named-intermediate alignment). (v2) addr-of-index store + return `*(&ot[0]) = mask; return &ot[0];` — score 6 (combine simplifies &ot[0] to ot identically). (v3) n-passthrough local `s32 nn = n; v0[11](ot, nn);` — score 6 (copy-prop folds nn to n before RTL gen). (v4) separated mask-block `{ u32 mask = ...; *ot = mask; }` (initializer form instead of separate decl+assigns) — score 6 (identical lowering; the inner-block scope doesn't change pseudo numbering). (v5) explicit `void(*disp)(u32*,s32) = ...; disp(ot,n);` (no inner brace block, fnptr local before call) — REGRESSED 6→14, build 36 vs target 38 (-2 insns, suggests combine collapsed dispatch differently AND the residual diverged structurally). (v6) inverted mask construction `mask = ~0xFF000000u;` — score 6 (combine folds the ~ to 0x00FFFFFF identical to direct 0xFFFFFF). (v7) inline v0 decl without inner brace block — score 6 (block scoping doesn't affect cc1's pseudo creation; documented in round 4 for similar form). PERMUTER (~12 min wallclock, j=4, seed=20260604 from score-6 base): 500+ iters explored, ZERO sub-baseline (135 weighted) candidates saved. All explored mutations scored ≥135 (baseline) and were rejected. Confirms randomization-only permuter on this base does not find lever — consistent with the cumulative ~50k iters across sessions 5-9 finding zero improvements. CUMULATIVE EVIDENCE across 8 grinding contexts (3 sessions + 2 workflow rounds + round-3 + round-4 + round-5 + round-6): ~80+ structural variants, ~50.5k+ permuter iters, ZERO progress beyond floor 6 in any context. The mechanistic ceiling (sched.c INSN_PRIORITY 1 vs 4 wall; return-staging chain depth must extend to >=4 without emitting bytes or copy-prop folding to ot) is corroborated by every single tested variant: every form either (i) folds via copy-prop / combine simplification (no priority change), (ii) emits extra bytes (regression), or (iii) introduces a semantically-wrong write offset (regression + wrong bytes). NEXT-SESSION CONCRETE ACTIONS (per `.claude/rules/no-new-park-categories.md` standing posture 'no new park category for register-rotation / scheduling-priority walls'; per `.claude/rules/difficult-is-not-impossible.md` 'matching C exists, keep grinding'): (1) directed-PERM_GENERAL permuter with explicit `// PERM_GENERAL(...)` macro annotation around the post-call statements — randomization permuter is exhausted but PERM_GENERAL targets specific RTL transforms; (2) instrumented BB2_SCHED_DEBUG dump on each of session-10's 7 new variants to see WHY each failed at the RTL level (would surface whether any approach the chain-depth wall from a different angle than the documented gp_v0 base allocation); (3) sibling cross-reference of OTHER pure-C-matched thin GPU vtable wrappers in display.c — gpu_ClearOTag (177) and gpu_SendPacket (210) — to extract their precise RTL-emission shapes for comparison.

remaining_gap.summary: 6 register-rotation + scheduling diffs in the final-block mask/addr chain. cc1 picks $v0 for the mask (target $a0); return-staging emits AFTER the store (target BEFORE). Mechanism: sched.c return-staging INSN_PRIORITY = 1 vs mask/addr chain priority = 4. To flip the order, the return-staging pseudo's chain depth must extend to >=4. No pure-C SOTN-allowed construct found that extends it without emitting bytes (every tested `u32 *p = ot; *p = ...; return p;` form folds to ot via copy-prop).
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: 6 regfix rules calibrated for HEAD's emission. The candidate's score-6 shape produces a different emission that doesn't byte-match; the rules would need rewriting for the new shape with no net rule reduction. Same lateral-movement reason as C7A0 / C86C.

next_hypotheses: ROUND-7 NEXT-SESSION ACTIONS (2026-06-05, post-v2-research): the v2 research enumerated 5 RTL-derived angles; only Angle 1 (`&ot` addressable spill) was net-new and self-vet-survivable, and measurement regressed it 6→19 plus surfaced a family-check failure (same intent as `(void)&local;` from .claude/rules/dead-vars-local-array.md scalar). Angles 2 (indirection-load via existing vtable), 4 (debug-branch return-value carry with `& 0` fold), 5 (fn-ptr return-type lie) were all self-vetted as cheat-by-spelling at design time. Concrete remaining actions: (1) **BB2_SCHED_DEBUG instrumented dump on the Angle-1 (`&ot`) form** — even though the form is a cheat-by-spelling, the SCHED dump may reveal whether the addressable-ot REG_DEP_TRUE rearrangement crosses a chain a non-cheat construct could exploit. The `move v0,s0` insn disappearing is a structural shift not seen in any prior session's RTL dumps. (2) **Sibling cross-reference of gpu_SendPacket (display.c:210, byte-matched)** — gpu_SendPacket has a post-call store (`((void(*)(u32*,u32))dev[5])(a0+4, size)`) but no return-staged ot; diffing its .sched dump against B844's would test whether ANY post-call-store-bearing matched sibling exhibits a transferable pattern. (3) **PERM_GENERAL directed permuter with explicit `// PERM_GENERAL(stmts)` macro annotations** around the post-call statements — narrower search-space class than the randomization permuter now exhausted at ~50.5k iters; targeted at the return-staging pseudo's REG_DEP_TRUE list per round-2's PRIORITY-3 hypothesis. Per .claude/rules/no-new-park-categories.md the standing answer for register-rotation / scheduling-priority walls is 'keep searching, no new infrastructure carve-out'. Per .claude/rules/difficult-is-not-impossible.md: matching C exists — continue grinding.

ROUND-5 PARK_CANDIDATE ESCALATION (2026-06-04, SOTN-research-driven): Session 9 executed the fresh-SOTN-research lever set (`tmp/sotn_research_func_8007B844.md`). Direct master-tree evidence search found NO SOTN precedent for the BB2 target shape (vtable dispatch + post-call `*ot = mask & 0xFFFFFF; return ot;`). SOTN's matched analogs either return immediately after the vtable call (DMA path — terminator written by vtable callee) or wrap the tail in a real `while (--n)` loop body (non-DMA path — loop body provides chain depth naturally). The empirically-tested research-proposed levers (ot[n-1] = mask: sandbox 9, ot[n] = mask: sandbox 8) BOTH regress because they require offset arithmetic AND semantically write to the wrong slot (target writes 0x0($v0), not (n-1)*4 or n*4). The research-proposed degenerate `do { } while (--_n);` lever is forbidden by `.claude/rules/do-while-zero-exception.md` (sched.c is not the LABEL_OUTSIDE_LOOP_P/reorg.c mechanism the exception sanctions; using it for sched.c is explicitly forbidden by the rule). EVIDENCE-EXHAUSTION SUMMARY (~73+ structural variants × 7 grinding contexts + ~50k permuter iters across 8 sessions): SOTN-allowed catalog (variable-reuse, opaque-arithmetic, sub-word reads, mixed-exit, duplicate-read, named-intermediate, sibling-mimicry, narrow-byte-args, deferred-store) = NEGATIVE. Sibling-mimicry against matched gpu_ClearOTag = NEGATIVE (decisive diff: same C tail produces DIFFERENT asm in B844 vs gpu_ClearOTag; preceding-body shape is the discriminator and cannot be matched without a loop, but target has no loop). Instrumented-cc1-derived levers from sched.c PRIORITY_DEBUG = NEGATIVE (no pure-C construct extends return-staging chain depth without emitting bytes or copy-prop-folding). Single un-attempted lever: directed-PERM_GENERAL permuter; randomization permuter already exhausted at 50k iters with zero gains, directed search would be over mutations within the same exhausted space. ESCALATION REQUIRES USER POLICY DECISION on one of: (a) accept indefinite park status under documented mechanistic ceilings as 'INCOMPLETE awaiting future toolchain or methodology breakthrough' — DEFAULT for the cluster (cpu_side_move_dir_4, marionation_Exec, saEft00Add already there); (b) sanction a NEW SOTN-evidence-backed exception class for thin-vtable-wrapper sched.c-priority walls — REQUIRES additional research finding SOTN-master-tree evidence the 2026-06-04 pass did NOT find; the agent does NOT recommend this category and explicitly does NOT propose it as evidence-backed; (c) authorize a permuter budget for one final directed-PERM run before park-acceptance. Per `.claude/rules/no-new-park-categories.md` the standing answer for register-rotation / scheduling-priority walls is 'keep searching, no new infrastructure carve-out'; this escalation surfaces the cumulative-evidence reality without requesting a category change. PRESERVE user_policy_note: canonical-asm authorization NOT evidence-backed for this function (S1=S2=S6=S8=0); no SOTN precedent for canonical-asm on a thin GPU vtable wrapper of this shape.

Workflow round 3 (2026-06-03) ESCALATION CANDIDATE — empirically exhausted across 5 distinct grinding contexts (3 sessions + 2 workflow rounds + round-3): ~70+ structural variants and ~50k+ permuter iters, ZERO progress beyond floor 6. Structural ceiling identified mechanistically (sched.c INSN_PRIORITY 1 vs 4 wall, no combine-foldable C construct creates the required REG_DEP_TRUE edge between return-staging and store-base pseudos). RECOMMENDED for round 4 if continuing autonomously: directed-PERM permuter (`PERM_*` macros) from the score-6 base targeting the return-staging pseudo's REG_DEP_TRUE list — untried in any prior round and noted in meta.json's WORKFLOW ROUND 2 NEXT HYPOTHESES as PRIORITY-3. The randomization-only permuter has ~50k cumulative iters with zero improvements; directed PERM macros are a different search-space class. Alternative: B844 joins the 'documented structural ceiling at sched.c priority wall' cluster as cpu_side_move_dir_4, marionation_Exec, saEft00Add — all parked indefinitely under no-new-park-categories policy, waiting on either a fundamentally novel SOTN-allowed pure-C technique discovered via future borderline-rule research OR user policy decision on the cluster. PRESERVE user_policy_note: canonical-asm authorization NOT evidence-backed for this function (S1=S2=S6=S8=0).

WORKFLOW ROUND 2 NEXT HYPOTHESES (2026-06-02): PRIORITY-1 — synthesize a degenerate-loop body wrapping the vtable-call so cc1 sees a loop-shape preceding-body matching gpu_ClearOTag's structure. The round-1 sibling-mimicry hypothesis was deprioritized because gpu_ClearOTag's tail matches our score-6 shape (not target's), but that conclusion has a subtle flaw: gpu_ClearOTag's tail emits AFTER a do-while loop that mutates ot via `a0 = next` inside. The loop ITSELF re-binds ot in a way the simple func_8007B844 form doesn't. If the loop-rebinding (not just the loop's existence) is what makes the tail emit a particular shape, then a synthetic degenerate `do { } while (0);` won't help — but an `if (n_unused-- > 0) ot = ot;` style loop-counter-decrement that genuinely USES n (in a way that survives DCE without emitting net bytes) might. NB: this risks the no-new-park-categories family if `ot = ot` is the rebinding mechanism. Worth instrumenting BB2_SCHED_DEBUG on gpu_ClearOTag's tail vs B844's tail to compare predecessor lists at the .greg/.sched dump level. PRIORITY-2 — examine whether ARG-SAVE prologue scheduling affects the post-call $s0/$v0 allocation choice. The prologue saves $s0/$s1 + arg-saves into them BEFORE the if(debug>=2) branch. cc1's allocator sees `ot` (in $s0) as live from prologue through entire function. Perturbing the prologue's arg-save sequence (by changing which arg is referenced first in body) might shift pseudo-creation-order in a way that flips the store-base allocation. Untried; risk: the arg-save reorder via local renames IS the param-local-alias-prologue-pair-flip cheat (forbidden); legitimate only if accomplished by reordering function's reads of ot vs n. PRIORITY-3 — PERM_GENERAL directed permuter from score-6 base with explicit direction 'extend return-staging chain depth without emitting bytes'. Randomization permuter has ~50k iters cumulative; PERM_GENERAL has not been targeted at return-staging pseudo's REG_DEP_TRUE list. Lowest confidence, lowest risk. EXPLICIT NON-AVENUES: function-pointer return-type lie (rejected_forms[1], FORBIDDEN), conditional dead-store family (FORBIDDEN by detector), volatile-on-local-pointer rebinding (open-class cheat by intent test), register-asm pin on `ot` post-call (cheat-asm pin, FORBIDDEN), canonical-asm authorization (NOT evidence-backed; all S1-S8 signals = 0).

ORCHESTRATOR CORRECTION 2026-06-02 (post-round-1): tail-bytes diff EXECUTED between gpu_ClearOTag (8007B814-8007B828) and func_8007B844 (8007B8A8-8007B8C0). The tails do NOT match — gpu_ClearOTag emits store-then-move (mask in $v1, addr-into-$v0, sw $v0 to 0($s0), addu $v0,$s0,$zero); B844 target emits move-then-store with $v0 as store base (mask in $a0, addu $v0,$s0,$zero, addr-into-$v1, sw $v1 to 0($v0)). KEY MECHANISTIC FINDING: B844 target uses the RETURN-STAGED $v0 as the store base address. cc1 needs to be steered such that the OT pointer's POST-CALL allocation lands the store base in $v0 (not $s0) — equivalent to: cc1 must keep $s0 free during the store-base materialisation. The 'sibling-mimicry degenerate-loop' hypothesis was WRONG-DIRECTION (gpu_ClearOTag's loop emits store-then-move, our current score-6 shape). The 'masked-6 hides 0-distance' hypothesis was FALSE (tails are byte-different). New top hypothesis: instrumented BB2_SCHED_DEBUG dump on the score-6 form to identify the predecessors of the store insn vs the return-staging insn — find what makes the store-base materialisation depend on $v0 (return-value reg) rather than $s0 (saved-arg reg). Then derive a non-cheat C structure that triggers that dependency. CAUTION: function-pointer return-type lie variants are FORBIDDEN (rejected_forms[1]).

Sibling-mimicry hypothesis (DEPRIORITIZED — tail diff shows WRONG DIRECTION): rewrite the vtable-call block as a degenerate loop mirroring gpu_ClearOTag's preceding-body structure. The tail-byte diff (above) shows gpu_ClearOTag's tail matches our SCORE-6 form, not B844's target. So mimicking gpu_ClearOTag's body would keep us at score 6, not close to 0. Demoted from top-of-list.

Fresh SOTN borderline-research pass — direct master-branch evidence search for patterns that affect sched.c return-staging priority. Categories: thin vtable-dispatch wrappers, OT-link builders, debug-conditional-call patterns. The 2026-06-02 borderline research found 6 NEW SOTN-allowed techniques; another pass may surface more.

BB2_SCHED_DEBUG instrumented cc1 dump on the candidate's RTL — identify the return-staging pseudo's REG_DEP_TRUE predecessors. The closing lever must extend that chain WITHOUT emitting bytes (combine-foldable) AND without falling into the no-new-park-categories family.

Sibling cross-reference — find another thin GPU vtable-dispatch wrapper (gpu_LinkList-style) that matches pure-C, diff its .greg dump against this one's. The natural C structure that makes return-staging high enough may already exist in a sibling.
Combined m2c re-read of target — m2c's faithful reconstruction may suggest a control-flow shape (e.g. early-return path that the worker hasn't tested) that affects scheduling at the RTL level.

rejected_forms.form: Single-statement mask: mask = ((u32)&g_gpu_ot_end) & 0xFFFFFF; *ot = mask;
rejected_forms.reason: Merging the two `mask =` statements regresses sandbox 6 -> 7. The named-intermediate split (Lever B) is load-bearing; fusion loses the addr-register $v1 alignment that closes the addr-register diff. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: Hoist mask init before call: `u32 mask = 0xFFFFFF;` at function entry, live across the vtable call.
rejected_forms.reason: Massive regression sandbox 6 -> 19. cc1 keeps mask in a callee-save reg across the call, inflating prologue/epilogue cost. The 0xFFFFFF materialization must happen AFTER the call. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: Explicit return-value variable: `u32 *ret = ot; ...; *ret = mask; return ret;`
rejected_forms.reason: Copy-prop folds ret back to ot; RTL is identical to the baseline. Sandbox 6, no progress and no detectable codegen change. Same outcome as documented in candidate.c's commentary. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: Comma-operator return: `return *ot = mask, ot;` (collapse store + return into one expression)
rejected_forms.reason: Comma operator gets flattened during RTL-gen; produces identical RTL to the baseline statement form. Sandbox 6, no semantic difference for cc1's optimizer. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: Split mask and stored value: `u32 mask, val; mask = 0xFFFFFF; val = ((u32)&g_gpu_ot_end) & mask; *ot = val;`
rejected_forms.reason: Introducing a second-name val regresses 6 -> 7. The single-name mask reuse across the AND and the store is what holds the addr-register in $v1 matching target; renaming the stored value breaks that. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: Sibling-asm cross-reference: gpu_ClearOTag (display.c:177, ALREADY matched) has the SAME tail C and emits return-staging-AFTER-store, mirroring our score-6 form.
rejected_forms.reason: Decisive sibling EVIDENCE (not a rejected form per se, recorded here for the next session): gpu_ClearOTag emits `lui/addiu D_8009BF30; and; sw $v0,0($s0); addu $v0,$s0,$zero` — exactly the score-6 shape we produce for B844, and gpu_ClearOTag is byte-matched. The same C tail compiles to a matching shape there. The discriminating factor is the function's preceding-body shape (single vtable call vs gpu_ClearOTag's loop), not the tail's C structure. Drives the new sibling-mimicry next_hypothesis. (Workflow round 1, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: conditional dead-store: `if (debug >= 2) { ...; new_var = ot; } ...; new_var = ot; *new_var = mask; return new_var;`
rejected_forms.reason: FORBIDDEN per .claude/rules/no-new-park-categories.md + commit 44ef3df (find_dead_conditional_stores detector). Same family as Lever D (dead-param-assign, forbidden 2026-05-31). The inner conditional store is DCE'd; its only effect is to influence RTL pseudo numbering for sched.c priority. Permuter-found across ~36k iters; vetted by the worker per cheats-by-any-spelling and NOT surfaced as a candidate.

rejected_forms.form: function-pointer return-type lie: declare the vtable entry's return type to coerce $v0 liveness through the call
rejected_forms.reason: Cheat-by-spelling — falsely declaring a function pointer's return type to manipulate GCC's value-flow analysis. The actual called function does not return that type; the declaration is a lie that exists solely for codegen control. Same family as inline-asm-policy expanded catalog. Not committed; tested only to rule out.

rejected_forms.form: ot[0] = mask; (round 2: array-index store at idx [0] instead of dereference)
rejected_forms.reason: Semantically and syntactically equivalent to *ot=mask; lowers to identical RTL. Sandbox 6 (no change). Documents an attempted re-spelling of the existing form — fails to differentiate the store insn's base-register allocation. Not a new lever, just a confirmation that surface syntax doesn't reach the underlying RTL choice. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: u32 *out_ptr; ... out_ptr = ot; *out_ptr = mask; return out_ptr; (round 2: post-call pointer rebinding via separately-named local)
rejected_forms.reason: Copy-prop folds out_ptr back to ot before RTL gen; emitted RTL is identical to baseline. Sandbox 6 (no change). Same family as the prior round's `u32 *ret = ot;` rebinding, but spelled with declaration FIRST and assignment AFTER the call. Confirms that variable name and assignment timing don't defeat copy-prop. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: u32 addr; ... mask = 0xFFFFFF; addr = (u32)&g_gpu_ot_end; *ot = addr & mask; (round 2: split-name mask + addr as separate locals, AND in store expression)
rejected_forms.reason: Regresses 6 -> 7. The named-intermediate `mask = ((u32)&g_gpu_ot_end) & mask;` form is load-bearing for addr-register $v1 alignment; splitting mask AND addr into two locals with the AND fused into the store expression breaks Lever B's effect. Essentially the val-split form under a different name, confirming the named-intermediate constraint is tight. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: u32 *p; ... p = ot + 0; *p = mask; return p; (round 2: pointer-arithmetic identity to attempt defeat of copy-prop)
rejected_forms.reason: Combine simplifies `ot + 0` to `ot` before flow.c; p is then copy-prop-folded to ot like all other rebinding forms. Sandbox 6 (no change). Confirms that algebraic identities GCC's combine can fold are not viable carriers for surviving rebinding — any new lever in this family must use an expression combine CANNOT fold to ot. (Workflow round 2, 2026-06-02.)

rejected_forms.form: u32 *ret; ... ret = ot; *ret = mask; return ret; (round 3: declaration FIRST, assignment after both calls)
rejected_forms.reason: Copy-prop folds ret to ot. Sandbox 6 (no change). Same lowering as the prior `u32 *ret = ot;` and `u32 *out_ptr;` forms; round-3 confirms that the declaration-vs-assignment ORDER doesn't matter for copy-prop. RTL gen sees ret as a copyable pseudo regardless of where the assignment statement sits. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: mask = (u32)ot; return (u32 *)mask; (round 3: SOTN-allowed variable reuse — reuse `mask` for return value per defeat-licm-hoist-var-reuse)
rejected_forms.reason: Regresses 6 -> 7. The SOTN-allowed variable-reuse technique requires a loop-invariant hoist to defeat (where multi-set defeats single-set movable). func_8007B844 has NO loop — no hoist mechanism exists. Reusing mask just breaks the named-intermediate alignment that Lever B established (sandbox 7). Confirms the SOTN variable-reuse technique is context-specific to loop-invariant defeat, not a general codegen lever. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: volatile u32 mask; (round 3: volatile qualifier on the LOCAL mask, not the global)
rejected_forms.reason: Rejected at design time per cheats-by-any-spelling intent test. A volatile-qualified local variable holding a constant 0xFFFFFF mask has ZERO semantic purpose — there's no concurrent access, no signal handler, no memory-mapped hardware. Same family as the inline `volatile T *` cast cheat catalog entry, just spelled with a local declaration qualifier. NOT MEASURED — vetted out before sandbox. (Workflow round 3, 2026-06-03.)

rejected_forms.form: direct vtable cast (round 4): `((void(**)(u32 *,s32))g_gpu_dev_table)[11](ot, n);` — collapses the `u32 *v0 = g_gpu_dev_table; ((void(*)(u32 *,s32))v0[11])(ot, n);` two-statement form into one expression
rejected_forms.reason: Combine folds the cast-then-index-deref-call chain to identical RTL as the explicit-`v0` intermediate. Same number of pseudos at the post-call boundary, same callee-save allocation for `ot` ($s0), same return-staging chain depth. Sandbox 6, no change. Confirms that dispatch-load surface syntax (intermediate local vs inline cast) does not affect the post-call dataflow shape that drives sched.c's priority decision. (Round 4, 2026-06-04, HEAD 57c65477.)

rejected_forms.form: SOTN-research lever 1a (round 5, 2026-06-04): `ot[n-1] = mask;` — write terminator to the LAST slot rather than slot 0, per tmp/sotn_research_func_8007B844.md's first ranked suggestion
rejected_forms.reason: Regresses 6 -> 9. Requires `(n-1)*4` offset arithmetic: `sll $tmp,$n,2; addu $tmp,$tmp,$ot; addiu $tmp,$tmp,-4` (+2 insns). Also semantically writes to byte offset (n-1)*4, not 0 — target asm writes `sw $v1, 0x0($v0)` (verified via asm/funcs/func_8007B844.s line containing `000043AC`). Cannot byte-match target by construction. The research file's hypothesis that 'this changes which pseudo cc1 must schedule' is correct surface-wise but the store base still depends on $ot (the input), NOT $v0 (the return value), so the required REG_DEP_TRUE edge between return-staging and store-base pseudos is not created. Confirms SOTN research's analytical hypothesis but rules out lever as a viable closure.

rejected_forms.form: SOTN-research lever 1b (round 5, 2026-06-04): `ot[n] = mask;` — write terminator to slot n rather than slot 0
rejected_forms.reason: Regresses 6 -> 8. Same semantic mismatch as lever 1a (target writes offset 0), with one fewer offset-arithmetic insn (no -4 addiu): `sll $tmp,$n,2; addu $tmp,$tmp,$ot` (+1 insn). Same mechanistic failure (store base depends on $ot, not $v0). Confirms research file's per-variant ranking (1b cheaper than 1a) but neither closes the priority gap.

rejected_forms.form: SOTN-research lever 2 (round 5, 2026-06-04, REJECTED AT DESIGN TIME): degenerate `do { *ot = mask; } while (--_n);` with `_n = 1;` (or literal `do { *ot = mask; } while (0);`)
rejected_forms.reason: Rejected on POLICY grounds without measurement. The `do { } while (0);` literal form is sanctioned ONLY by `.claude/rules/do-while-zero-exception.md` for the LABEL_OUTSIDE_LOOP_P / reorg.c invert-jump peephole mechanism, NOT for sched.c's INSN_PRIORITY chain depth (the documented mechanism here). The rule explicitly states 'Using it to bend other GCC passes reopens the slippery-slope this rule exists to close.' The non-literal `while (--_n);` form either unrolls to identical baseline RTL (no priority effect, single-iter known constant) or emits a back-branch (regression); the introduced `_n = 1;` variable has no semantic purpose (would fail the cheat-reviewer's family check) — same intent as the dead-conditional-store / unused-local-array family. Cheat-reviewer would FAIL the form.

rejected_forms.form: v2-research Angle 1 (round 7, 2026-06-05): `g_gpu_debug_func(&D_80015F98, &ot, n)` — make `ot` addressable by passing its address to the varargs debug helper
rejected_forms.reason: Regresses 6 -> 19. Mechanism worked partially as v2 research predicted: addressable `ot` is given a stack home, the `move v0,s0` return-staging insn becomes unnecessary (-1 insn vs target 38), but the schedule of every other insn cascades differently and the masked diff explodes. ALSO fails cheats-by-any-spelling intent check: passing `&ot` (a u32**) to a debug helper expecting `ot` (u32*) is a semantic mismatch with no human-programmer justification — sole purpose is forcing addressability for codegen control, same family intent as `(void)&local;` from .claude/rules/dead-vars-local-array.md scalar variant (spelled inside a function call argument instead of a void-cast statement). Cheat-reviewer would FAIL the form. Measured to confirm v2 research's analytical hypothesis (mostly regresses) and to rule out lever empirically.

rejected_forms.form: Post-increment + arithmetic rebinding (round 7, 2026-06-05): `u32 *ret = ot; *ret++ = mask; return ret - 1;`
rejected_forms.reason: Combine folds `*ret++ = mask; return ret - 1;` to identical RTL as baseline `*ot = mask; return ot;`. The `ret` pseudo is copy-prop-folded to `ot`, then combine simplifies the +1/-1 pointer arithmetic identity. Sandbox 6 (no change). Joins the rejected-forms family of pointer-rebinding variants (round 1 `u32 *ret = ot`, round 2 `out_ptr = ot`, round 2 `p = ot + 0`, round 3 `u32 *ret declaration-first`) — every form that introduces a fresh ptr pseudo equal to ot ultimately folds. Confirms even non-trivial arithmetic identities (post-increment + -1 offset) are within combine's simplification reach.

rejected_forms.form: Reverse named-intermediate order (round 10, 2026-06-05): `mask = (u32)&g_gpu_ot_end; mask = mask & 0xFFFFFF; *ot = mask;` — addr loaded first, then masked, vs Lever B's const-first form `mask = 0xFFFFFF; mask = ((u32)&g_gpu_ot_end) & mask; *ot = mask;`
rejected_forms.reason: Regresses 6 -> 7. The Lever B form requires the CONST to be staged into the mask pseudo FIRST, then the address-load to AND-fold into the SAME pseudo — this is what aligns the addr-register to target's $v1. Reversing the order makes cc1 stage the address-load into a fresh pseudo (later renamed via the second assignment), causing the addr to land in a different register and breaking the alignment. Confirms even superficial 2-statement reordering of the same operands breaks Lever B's specific operand evaluation chain; the const-first form is uniquely correct among the variants in this neighbourhood. Joins the score-7 rejected-forms family (single-statement mask fuse, val-split, split-name addr-then-AND-in-store) — all share the broken-Lever-B-alignment failure mode. (Round 10, 2026-06-05, HEAD a5b2d97a.)

rejected_forms.form: Fused-AND-into-store with retained mask init (round 11, 2026-06-06): `u32 mask; mask = 0xFFFFFF; *ot = ((u32)&g_gpu_ot_end) & mask; return ot;` — keeps Lever B's first statement (`mask = 0xFFFFFF;`) but eliminates the second rebinding (`mask = ((u32)&g_gpu_ot_end) & mask;`) by inlining the AND into the store RHS
rejected_forms.reason: Regresses 6 -> 7. Mechanism: removing the rebinding statement removes the named-pseudo target for the AND result. cc1 allocates the AND result to a fresh pseudo (not `mask`'s register), which breaks the addr-register $v1 alignment that Lever B established. Distinct from rejected_forms[0] (single-statement mask fuse fuses BOTH mask= statements into one expression `mask = addr & 0xFFFFFF;`); this form keeps the first init statement intact but eliminates the rebinding. Confirms that the named-intermediate REBINDING (not just the named-intermediate's existence) is what holds Lever B's specific addr-register alignment. The mask-init statement is necessary but NOT sufficient — the rebinding into mask's pseudo is ALSO load-bearing. Together with the round-10 finding, pins Lever B's structure as TIGHTLY constrained: exactly 3 statements (mask=CONST; mask=ADDR&mask; *ot=mask;) with no fusion, no reordering, no rebinding-elimination tolerated. Joins the score-7 rejected-forms cluster. (Round 11, 2026-06-06, HEAD d4872471.)

memory_notes: memory/project/func-8007b844-conditional-dead-store.md (the full session 2 ledger; documents both the score-6 lever and the rejected score-0 cheat)

instrumented_evidence.finding: return-staging insn INSN_PRIORITY = 1 (REG_DEP_TRUE -> jr ra only, chain depth 1). mask/addr chain INSN_PRIORITY = 4 (lui->addiu->and->sw, chain depth 4). rank_for_schedule picks higher priority first; mask/addr emits before return-staging.
instrumented_evidence.closing_lever_requirement: Extend return-staging chain depth to >=4 via C-source dependencies that combine-fold to zero emitted bytes. No SOTN-allowed pattern in the current catalog accomplishes this; all tested forms either fold (no priority change) or materialize (cost exceeds benefit).

user_policy_note: Worker session 3 suggested 'canonical-asm authorization OR a fresh SOTN borderline-research pass' as next-step options. The canonical-asm option is NOT evidence-backed — B844's target asm has ZERO hand-coded signals (S1=0 'handwritten' annotations; S2=none hardcoded register signatures; S6=standard ABI; S8=no redundant-mask-before-shift). Every instruction has a direct C analog; no GTE/BIOS/scratchpad. Per .claude/rules/hand-coded-asm-recognition.md and .claude/rules/no-compiler-divergence.md, the structural ceiling is a sched.c INSN_PRIORITY wall same class as cpu_side_move_dir_4 / marionation_Exec — not hand-coded asm. Keep parked + WIP'd; continue legitimate lever search.
prior_sessions_summary: 14 sessions 2026-05-31..2026-06-06 + workflow rounds: floor 7 (session1, 32 variants) -> 6 (session2, Lever B named-intermediate mask) and STABLE at 6 through 50+ structural variants + ~50k permuter iters. Instrumented (BB2_SCHED_DEBUG): target needs return-staging `move $v0,$s0` at idx 23 BEFORE the mask/addr chain (idx24-26); cc1 sched.c gives return-staging INSN_PRIORITY=1 vs chain priority=4. No pure-C SOTN-allowed construct extends the return-staging pseudo's chain depth (every `u32 *p=ot; *p=...; return p;` copy-prop-folds to ot). Sibling gpu_ClearOTag (byte-matched, display.c:177) has the IDENTICAL C tail and also emits return-staging AFTER the store at score-6 emission order — so score-6 is a legitimate matching shape; the discriminator is body shape (single vtable call here vs ClearOTag's loop), not the tail. Rejected forms: conditional dead-store + fnptr return-type-lie (both FORBIDDEN).
